untranslatable_reason: This signal describes an options strategy (diagonal backspread) that requires multi-leg option parameters (strike prices, expiration dates, option types, quantity ratios) which cannot be expressed using only the allowed price/technical indicators. The strategy involves: (1) selling in-the-money near-term options, (2) buying greater quantity of longer-term LEAPS at/out-of-the-money calls, and (3) dynamic rolling based on underlying price movement. These are options chain mechanics, not price-based technical conditions. Required parameters: option_strike_relationship, option_expiration_relationship, option_quantity_ratio, and option_type_selector are not in the allowed indicator list.
translation_notes: Signal MCMILLAN_DRY_179 is a structured options spread strategy from McMillan's work. It is not translatable to equity price action rules. This requires an options-specific backtesting engine with access to the options chain (strikes, expirations, greeks, bid-ask data). The signal is directionally neutral/context-dependent and involves active management (rolling) which further complicates translation to static technical rules.
